description: Students implement seam carving: energy map via Sobel convolution, minimum-energy seam via DP, seam removal, and full pipeline.

test_files.toy_image.npy: A tiny 8x6x3 float32 image where column 2 is uniform (lowest energy)
test_files.grading_test_image.jpg: Real photo (kids running) for visual grading tests
test_files.test_image.jpg: Fallback image for student notebook compatibility

reference_code:
def _ref_compute_energy(gray):
    H, W = gray.shape
    sx = torch.tensor([[-1.,0.,1.],[-2.,0.,2.],[-1.,0.,1.]])
    sy = torch.tensor([[-1.,-2.,-1.],[0.,0.,0.],[1.,2.,1.]])
    gx = conv2d(gray, sx)
    gy = conv2d(gray, sy)
    e = gx.abs() + gy.abs()
    return F.pad(e.unsqueeze(0), (1, 1, 1, 1), mode='replicate').squeeze(0)

def _ref_find_seam(energy):
    H, W = energy.shape
    M = torch.zeros_like(energy)
    M[0] = energy[0]
    backtrack = torch.zeros(H, W, dtype=torch.long)
    for i in range(1, H):
        left = F.pad(M[i-1, :W-1].unsqueeze(0), (1, 0), value=float('inf')).squeeze(0)
        right = F.pad(M[i-1, 1:].unsqueeze(0), (0, 1), value=float('inf')).squeeze(0)
        center = M[i-1]
        stacked = torch.stack([left, center, right], dim=0)
        vals, dirs = stacked.min(dim=0)
        M[i] = energy[i] + vals
        backtrack[i] = dirs - 1  # -1, 0, or 1 offset
    seam = torch.zeros(H, dtype=torch.long)
    seam[-1] = M[-1].argmin()
    for i in range(H-2, -1, -1):
        seam[i] = seam[i+1] + backtrack[i+1, seam[i+1]]
        seam[i] = seam[i].clamp(0, W-1)
    return seam

def _ref_remove_seam(img, seam):
    if img.dim() == 2:
        H, W = img.shape
        mask = torch.ones(H, W, dtype=torch.bool)
        for i in range(H):
            mask[i, seam[i]] = False
        return img[mask].reshape(H, W-1)
    else:
        H, W, C = img.shape
        mask = torch.ones(H, W, dtype=torch.bool)
        for i in range(H):
            mask[i, seam[i]] = False
        return img[mask.unsqueeze(-1).expand_as(img)].reshape(H, W-1, C)

def _ref_seam_carve(img, n_seams):
    result = img.clone()
    for k in range(n_seams):
        gray = to_grayscale(result)
        energy = _ref_compute_energy(gray)
        seam = _ref_find_seam(energy)
        result = _ref_remove_seam(result, seam)
    return result

compute_energy.description: Compute gradient magnitude energy map using Sobel filters. Must return same shape as input, padded.

tests.name: energy_shape
tests.check: assert result.shape == (20, 30), f'Energy shape {result.shape}, expected (20, 30)'
tests.description: Energy map must be same shape as input

tests.name: energy_nonneg
tests.check: assert (result >= -1e-6).all(), f'Energy has negative values: min = {result.min():.6f}'
tests.description: Energy values should be non-negative (absolute values of gradients)

tests.name: energy_correctness
tests.check: assert torch.allclose(result, expected, atol=1e-4), f'Max diff {(result - expected).abs().max():.6f}'
tests.description: Energy values match reference on random input

tests.name: energy_uniform
tests.check: assert result.max() < 1e-5, f'Uniform image should have ~zero energy, got max {result.max():.6f}'
tests.description: Uniform image should have near-zero energy everywhere

tests.name: energy_toy_image
tests.check:
assert result.shape == _toy_gray.shape, f'Shape {result.shape} != {_toy_gray.shape}'
assert torch.allclose(result, expected, atol=1e-4), f'Toy image energy wrong, max diff {(result - expected).abs().max():.6f}'
tests.description: Energy matches reference on toy image (8x6)

tests.name: energy_real_image
tests.check:
_save_comparison_png([_test_img, expected, result], ['Test Image', 'Reference Energy', 'Student Energy'], '__vis_energy__.png')
assert result.shape == _test_gray.shape, f'Shape {result.shape} != {_test_gray.shape}'
assert torch.allclose(result, expected, atol=1e-3), f'Real image energy wrong, max diff {(result - expected).abs().max():.6f}'
tests.description: Energy matches reference on real test image

find_seam.description: Find minimum-energy vertical seam using dynamic programming. Return column indices per row.

tests.name: seam_shape
tests.check:
assert result.shape == (20,), f'Seam shape {result.shape}, expected (20,)'
assert result.dtype in (torch.int64, torch.int32, torch.long), f'Seam dtype {result.dtype}, expected integer'
tests.description: Seam is a 1D integer tensor with one entry per row

tests.name: seam_connected
tests.check:
_diffs = (result[1:] - result[:-1]).abs()
assert (_diffs <= 1).all(), f'Seam not connected: max jump = {_diffs.max()}'
tests.description: Adjacent seam pixels must differ by at most 1 column

tests.name: seam_bounds
tests.check: assert (result >= 0).all() and (result < 20).all(), f'Seam out of bounds: min={result.min()}, max={result.max()}'
tests.description: All seam indices must be within image bounds

tests.name: seam_optimal
tests.check:
_cost_student = _energy[torch.arange(25), result].sum()
_cost_ref = _energy[torch.arange(25), expected].sum()
assert abs(_cost_student - _cost_ref) < 1e-4, f'Seam cost {_cost_student:.4f} != optimal {_cost_ref:.4f}'
tests.description: Seam must have optimal (minimum) total energy

tests.name: seam_trivial
tests.check: assert (result == 2).all(), f'On a trivial energy with zero column, seam should be all 2s, got {result}'
tests.description: When one column has zero energy, seam should follow it exactly

tests.name: seam_toy_image
tests.check:
_cost_s = _toy_energy[torch.arange(8), result].sum()
_cost_r = _toy_energy[torch.arange(8), expected].sum()
assert abs(_cost_s - _cost_r) < 1e-4, f'Toy image seam cost {_cost_s:.4f} != optimal {_cost_r:.4f}'
tests.description: Finds optimal seam on toy image

tests.name: seam_real_image
tests.check:
_H = _test_energy.shape[0]
_cost_s = _test_energy[torch.arange(_H), result].sum()
_cost_r = _test_energy[torch.arange(_H), expected].sum()
_vis_img = _test_img.clone()
for _i in range(_H):
    _vis_img[_i, result[_i]] = torch.tensor([1.,0.,0.])
_vis_ref = _test_img.clone()
for _i in range(_H):
    _vis_ref[_i, expected[_i]] = torch.tensor([0.,1.,0.])
_save_comparison_png([_test_img, _vis_ref, _vis_img], ['Test Image', 'Reference Seam (green)', 'Student Seam (red)'], '__vis_seam__.png')
assert abs(_cost_s - _cost_r) < 1e-3, f'Real image seam cost {_cost_s:.4f} != optimal {_cost_r:.4f}'
tests.description: Finds optimal seam on real test image

remove_seam.description: Remove a vertical seam from an image. Works on both (H,W) and (H,W,3).

tests.name: remove_shape_rgb
tests.check: assert result.shape == (10, 14, 3), f'Shape {result.shape}, expected (10, 14, 3)'
tests.description: Output is one pixel narrower (RGB)

tests.name: remove_shape_gray
tests.check: assert result.shape == (10, 14), f'Shape {result.shape}, expected (10, 14)'
tests.description: Output is one pixel narrower (grayscale)

tests.name: remove_correctness
tests.setup:
torch.manual_seed(9)
_img = torch.rand(12, 20, 3)
_seam = torch.randint(0, 20, (12,))
for i in range(1, 12):
    _seam[i] = (_seam[i-1] + torch.randint(-1, 2, (1,)).item()).clamp(0, 19)
tests.check: assert torch.allclose(result, expected, atol=1e-6), f'Max diff {(result - expected).abs().max():.6f}'
tests.description: Pixel values after removal match reference

tests.name: remove_real_image
tests.check:
assert result.shape == expected.shape, f'Shape {result.shape} vs {expected.shape}'
assert torch.allclose(result, expected, atol=1e-4), f'Max diff {(result - expected).abs().max():.6f}'
tests.description: Seam removal matches reference on real test image

seam_carve.description: Full pipeline: iteratively remove n seams from an RGB image.

tests.name: carve_shape
tests.check: assert result.shape == (30, 40, 3), f'Shape {result.shape}, expected (30, 40, 3)'
tests.description: After removing 10 seams, width should decrease by 10

tests.name: carve_correctness_small
tests.check:
assert result.shape == expected.shape, f'Shape {result.shape} vs {expected.shape}'
assert torch.allclose(result, expected, atol=1e-4), f'Max diff {(result - expected).abs().max():.6f}'
tests.description: Full pipeline output matches reference on small random image

tests.name: carve_preserves_range
tests.check:
assert result.min() >= -1e-6, f'Negative pixel values: min = {result.min():.6f}'
assert result.max() <= 1.0 + 1e-6, f'Pixel values > 1: max = {result.max():.6f}'
tests.description: Output pixel values stay in [0, 1]

tests.name: carve_real_image
tests.check:
_save_comparison_png([_test_img, expected, result], ['Original', 'Reference (100 seams removed)', 'Student (100 seams removed)'], '__vis_carve__.png')
assert result.shape == expected.shape, f'Shape {result.shape} vs {expected.shape}'
_diff = (result - expected).abs().max().item()
assert _diff < 1e-3, f'Real image carve differs from reference: max diff {_diff:.6f}'
tests.description: Full pipeline matches reference on real test image (100 seams)

common_mistakes.symptom: energy_shape fails — output is 2 pixels smaller in each dimension
common_mistakes.diagnosis: Student forgot to pad the energy map back to original size after convolution.

common_mistakes.symptom: energy_correctness fails but shape and non-negativity pass
common_mistakes.diagnosis: Student may have used wrong Sobel kernels, or forgot to take absolute values.
The DP recurrence or backtracking is wrong.
Seam is valid but not optimal.

common_mistakes.symptom: carve_correctness_small fails but individual functions pass
common_mistakes.diagnosis: The pipeline function doesn't correctly chain the steps.

grading_notes.partial_credit_policy: Each function is graded independently. A bug in compute_energy that cascades into seam_carve should only be penalized once (in compute_energy). If the pipeline is correct but a component has a small bug, give full pipeline credit if the logic is sound.
